sample.title: Why South Korea’s AI Stock Mania Is a Warning to the World
sample.description:
South Korea’s stock market has surged about 200% year-on-year, powered by retail investors chasing an artificial intelligence-fueled chip rally. But with extreme volatility and a heavy reliance on two companies, is this a sustainable boom or a bubble waiting to burst? 

For more on what's happening in stocks, bonds, currencies and commodities and what will move them next, subscribe here to Bloomberg Markets Daily.
https://www.bloomberg.com/account/newsletters/markets-daily

sample.title: Why McDonald's is supersizing in China
sample.description:
China is a major source of McDonald’s growth and it plans to have 10,000 locations in mainland China by the end of 2028.

CNBC's Eunice Yoon explains how the chain is winning over Chinese consumers with both value and nostalgia.

Find the full report in related video.

sample.title: This New Medicare Pilot Program Offers GLP-1s For $50 A Month. Here’s What You Need To Know
sample.description:
Americans on Medicare can now purchase GLP-1s for just $50 a month thanks to a temporary pilot program called Bridge.  Patients who meet certain requirements, like having  BMI of over 35 or having obesity-related conditions, may not qualify. CNBC’s Annika Constantino has more.

Produced, Shot and Edited by: Natalie Rice
Reporting by: Annika Constantino
Senior Managing Producer: Tala Hadavi
Additional Footage: Getty Images

sample.title: ICE Could Sign A $220M Contract With A Taser Maker That Trump Invested Millions In
sample.description:
In February 2026, President Trump invested up to $5 million in top U.S. taser-maker Axon Enterprise. Two weeks later, ICE announced that it was seeking a $220 million contract to more than quadruple its inventory. Experts say that Axon is the only company able to meet the requirement outlined by ICE. CNBC’s Luke Fountain breaks it down.

Reporter: Luke Fountain
Produced by: Macklin Fishman
Edited by: Andrea Miller
Animation: Jason Reginato, Emily Park
Shot by: Charlotte Morabito
Senior Managing Producer: Shawn Baldwin
Additional Footage: Getty Images

sample.title: Inside The Rise And Fall Of Kohl's
sample.description:
Kohl's rose to its peak as a department store in the 2000s, with a focus on a strong in-store experience, coupons and rewards. Now, after years of stagnant sales and a rough patch on Wall Street, Kohl's is trying to get back to what made it a household name.

Reporter: Laya Neelakandan
Produced by: Tasia Jensen

sample.title: Why Analysts Say The Auto Industry Is Heading For Demographic Cliff
sample.description:
The auto industry is staring at a 'perfect storm' of slowing population growth, longer lasting cars, high prices, and new technology. These factors stand to put significant downward pressure on new car sales by 2040, according to analysts at Bain & Co.. The landscape could be far more competitive and prone to consolidation.

Chapters:
00:00 Introduction
00:46 Chapter 1:  The reasons why 
03:22 Chapter 2:  Uncertainty 
04:56 Chapter 3: Competitive U.S. 

Reporter: Robert Ferris
Produced, shot, and edited by: Darren Geeter
Additional editing by: Carlos Waters
Animations: Jason Reginato, Emily Park 
Senior Managing Producer: Tala Hadavi

sample.title: 'Heat dome' threatens sweltering conditions for World Cup fans, players
sample.description:
'Heat dome' threatens sweltering conditions for World Cup fans, players

Extreme heat greets fans and players at the World Cup, as an imposing "heat dome" settles over the central and eastern United States and parts of Canada with the knockout rounds progressing.
 
The weather phenomenon – a large area of high pressure that traps heat and humidity – could lead to dangerously high ‌temperatures. Amy Tennery explains the impact on the tournament. 

#Heat #Weather #Climate #FIFA #WorldCup

sample.title: Supreme Court expands executive power, splits on Trump cases
sample.description:
The U.S. Supreme Court ended Donald Trump's quest to narrow who can be considered a citizen if born in the United States, a harsh loss for the Republican president. But just one day before, it handed him a form of power that the dissenting justices said even English monarchs of the past did not possess. Andrew Chung explains.
 
#SupremeCourt #Trump #UnitedStates #USA

sample.title: Colorado company's technology attracts defense contract
sample.description:
Colorado's FlackTek says its bladeless mixing technology slashes rocket-propellant production from hours to minutes, winning defense startup Anduril as a customer as the US races to boost missile-manufacturing capacity.
